title: Fix Internal Path Disclosure via Stack Traces
description:
**Penetration Test Finding — Medium Severity**

Internal file system paths were disclosed via stack traces on multiple endpoints. By sending malformed input (e.g., SQL injection characters in parameters), attackers could trigger error responses that revealed internal server paths, which could be used for Local File Inclusion (LFI) attacks.

## Affected Endpoints

- `/teams/add-more-users/` — adding a single quote to the `user_number` parameter triggered a stack trace
- `/object_meta/get-single-object` — requesting a non-existent `object_id` returned a 404 with internal paths

## Risk

Medium — disclosed paths provide reconnaissance for further attacks (LFI, path traversal).

## Fix

Disabled debug mode (`APP_DEBUG=false`) on staging to match production configuration. Production already suppressed stack traces.
acceptance_criteria: Stack traces are not exposed in any HTTP response on staging or production
Error responses return generic 500 Internal Server Error without internal paths
Application debug mode is disabled on all non-development environments
Malformed input to /teams/add-more-users/ and /object_meta/get-single-object returns safe error responses
implementation_notes: The issue was already mitigated on production (APP_DEBUG=false). The fix was to also disable debug mode on staging before the next penetration test to ensure a pass. Laravel's debug mode was set to off via environment configuration.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed production already suppresses stack traces. After discussion, debug mode was also disabled on staging to pass the next pen test cycle.
